#pragma once
// clang-format off

// --- Getters ---
#define FOR_EACH_GETTER(macro) \
    macro(Archive) \
    macro(CSRF) \
    macro(CryptoHasher) \
    macro(FFI) \
    macro(FileSystemRouter) \
    macro(Glob) \
    macro(JSON5) \
    macro(JSONC) \
    macro(MD4) \
    macro(markdown) \
    macro(MD5) \
    macro(S3Client) \
    macro(SHA1) \
    macro(SHA224) \
    macro(SHA256) \
    macro(SHA384) \
    macro(SHA512) \
    macro(SHA512_256) \
    macro(TOML) \
    macro(YAML) \
    macro(Terminal) \
    macro(Transpiler) \
    macro(ValkeyClient) \
    macro(argv) \
    macro(assetPrefix) \
    macro(cron) \
    macro(cwd) \
    macro(embeddedFiles) \
    macro(enableANSIColors) \
    macro(hash) \
    macro(inspect) \
    macro(origin) \
    macro(s3) \
    macro(semver) \
    macro(unsafe) \
    macro(valkey) \

// --- Callbacks ---
#define FOR_EACH_CALLBACK(macro) \
    macro(allocUnsafe) \
    macro(braces) \
    macro(build) \
    macro(color) \
    macro(connect) \
    macro(createParsedShellScript) \
    macro(createShellInterpreter) \
    macro(deflateSync) \
    macro(file) \
    macro(fs) \
    macro(gc) \
    macro(generateHeapSnapshot) \
    macro(gunzipSync) \
    macro(gzipSync) \
    macro(indexOfLine) \
    macro(inflateSync) \
    macro(jest) \
    macro(listen) \
    macro(mmap) \
    macro(nanoseconds) \
    macro(openInEditor) \
    macro(registerMacro) \
    macro(resolve) \
    macro(resolveSync) \
    macro(serve) \
    macro(sha) \
    macro(shellEscape) \
    macro(shrink) \
    macro(sleepSync) \
    macro(spawn) \
    macro(spawnSync) \
    macro(stringWidth) \
    macro(udpSocket) \
    macro(which) \
    macro(write) \
    macro(zstdCompressSync) \
    macro(zstdDecompressSync) \
    macro(zstdCompress) \
    macro(zstdDecompress) \

#define DECLARE_ZIG_BUN_OBJECT_CALLBACK(name) BUN_DECLARE_HOST_FUNCTION(BunObject_callback_##name);
FOR_EACH_CALLBACK(DECLARE_ZIG_BUN_OBJECT_CALLBACK);
#undef DECLARE_ZIG_BUN_OBJECT_CALLBACK

// declaration for the exported function in BunObject.zig
#define DECLARE_ZIG_BUN_OBJECT_GETTER(name) extern "C" JSC::EncodedJSValue SYSV_ABI BunObject_lazyPropCb_##name(JSC::JSGlobalObject*, JSC::JSObject*);
FOR_EACH_GETTER(DECLARE_ZIG_BUN_OBJECT_GETTER);
#undef DECLARE_ZIG_BUN_OBJECT_GETTER

// definition of the C++ wrapper to call the Zig function
#define DEFINE_ZIG_BUN_OBJECT_GETTER_WRAPPER(name) static JSC::JSValue BunObject_lazyPropCb_wrap_##name(JSC::VM &vm, JSC::JSObject *object) { \
    return JSC::JSValue::decode(BunObject_lazyPropCb_##name(object->globalObject(), object)); \
} \

FOR_EACH_GETTER(DEFINE_ZIG_BUN_OBJECT_GETTER_WRAPPER);
#undef DEFINE_ZIG_BUN_OBJECT_GETTER_WRAPPER

#undef FOR_EACH_GETTER
#undef FOR_EACH_CALLBACK

784	  namespace TOML {
785	    /**
786	     * Parse a TOML string into a JavaScript object.
787	     *
788	     * @category Utilities
789	     *
790	     * @param input The TOML string to parse
791	     * @returns A JavaScript object
792	     */
793	    export function parse(input: string): object;
794	  }
795	
796	  /**
797	   * JSONC related APIs
798	   */
799	  namespace JSONC {
800	    /**
801	     * Parse a JSONC (JSON with Comments) string into a JavaScript value.
802	     *
803	     * Supports both single-line (`//`) and block comments (`/* ... *\/`), as well as
804	     * trailing commas in objects and arrays.
805	     *
806	     * @category Utilities
807	     *
808	     * @param input The JSONC string to parse
809	     * @returns A JavaScript value
810	     *
811	     * @example
812	     * ```js
813	     * const result = Bun.JSONC.parse(`{
814	     *   // This is a comment
815	     *   "name": "my-app",
816	     *   "version": "1.0.0", // trailing comma is allowed
817	     * }`);
818	     * ```
819	     */
820	    export function parse(input: string): unknown;
821	  }
822	
823	  /**
824	   * JSONL (JSON Lines) related APIs.
825	   *
826	   * Each line in the input is expected to be a valid JSON value separated by newlines.
827	   */
828	  namespace JSONL {
829	    /**
830	     * The result of `Bun.JSONL.parseChunk`.
831	     */
832	    interface ParseChunkResult {
833	      /** The successfully parsed JSON values. */
834	      values: unknown[];
835	      /** How far into the input was consumed. When the input is a string, this is a character offset. When the input is a `TypedArray`, this is a byte offset. Use `input.slice(read)` or `input.subarray(read)` to get the unconsumed remainder. */
836	      read: number;
837	      /** `true` if all input was consumed successfully. `false` if the input ends with an incomplete value or a parse error occurred. */
838	      done: boolean;
839	      /** A `SyntaxError` if a parse error occurred, otherwise `null`. Values parsed before the error are still available in `values`. */
840	      error: SyntaxError | null;
841	    }
842	
843	    /**
844	     * Parse a JSONL (JSON Lines) string into an array of JavaScript values.
845	     *
846	     * If a parse error occurs and no values were successfully parsed, throws
847	     * a `SyntaxError`. If values were parsed before the error, returns the
848	     * successfully parsed values without throwing.
849	     *
850	     * Incomplete trailing values (e.g. from a partial chunk) are silently
851	     * ignored and not included in the result.
852	     *
853	     * When a `TypedArray` is passed, the bytes are parsed directly without
854	     * copying if the content is ASCII.
855	     *
856	     * @param input The JSONL string or typed array to parse
857	     * @returns An array of parsed values
858	     * @throws {SyntaxError} If the input starts with invalid JSON and no values could be parsed
859	     *
860	     * @example
861	     * ```js
862	     * const items = Bun.JSONL.parse('{"a":1}\n{"b":2}\n');
863	     * // [{ a: 1 }, { b: 2 }]

1410	  namespace JSON5 {
1411	    /**
1412	     * Parse a JSON5 string into a JavaScript value.
1413	     *
1414	     * JSON5 is a superset of JSON based on ECMAScript 5.1 that supports
1415	     * comments, trailing commas, unquoted keys, single-quoted strings,
1416	     * hex numbers, Infinity, NaN, and more.
1417	     *
1418	     * @category Utilities
1419	     *
1420	     * @param input The JSON5 string to parse
1421	     * @returns A JavaScript value
1422	     *
1423	     * @example
1424	     * ```ts
1425	     * import { JSON5 } from "bun";
1426	     *
1427	     * const result = JSON5.parse(`{
1428	     *   // This is a comment
1429	     *   name: 'my-app',
1430	     *   version: '1.0.0', // trailing comma is allowed
1431	     *   hex: 0xDEADbeef,
1432	     *   half: .5,
1433	     *   infinity: Infinity,
1434	     * }`);
1435	     * ```
1436	     */
1437	    export function parse(input: string): unknown;
1438	
1439	    /**
1440	     * Convert a JavaScript value into a JSON5 string. Object keys that are
1441	     * valid identifiers are unquoted, strings use double quotes, `Infinity`
1442	     * and `NaN` are represented as literals, and indented output includes
1443	     * trailing commas.
1444	     *
1445	     * @category Utilities
1446	     *
1447	     * @param input The JavaScript value to stringify.
1448	     * @param replacer Currently not supported.
1449	     * @param space A number for how many spaces each level of indentation gets, or a string used as indentation.
1450	     *              The number is clamped between 0 and 10, and the first 10 characters of the string are used.
1451	     * @returns A JSON5 string, or `undefined` if the input is `undefined`, a function, or a symbol.
1452	     *
1453	     * @example
1454	     * ```ts
1455	     * import { JSON5 } from "bun";
1456	     *
1457	     * console.log(JSON5.stringify({ a: 1, b: "two" }));
1458	     * // {a:1,b:"two"}
1459	     *
1460	     * console.log(JSON5.stringify({ a: 1, b: 2 }, null, 2));
1461	     * // {
1462	     * //   a: 1,
1463	     * //   b: 2,
1464	     * // }
1465	     * ```
1466	     */
1467	    export function stringify(input: unknown, replacer?: undefined | null, space?: string | number): string | undefined;
1468	  }
1469

file.content:
namespace JSON5 {
    /**
     * Parse a JSON5 string into a JavaScript value.
     *
     * JSON5 is a superset of JSON based on ECMAScript 5.1 that supports
     * comments, trailing commas, unquoted keys, single-quoted strings,
     * hex numbers, Infinity, NaN, and more.
     *
     * @category Utilities
     *
     * @param input The JSON5 string to parse
     * @returns A JavaScript value
     *
     * @example
     * ```ts
     * import { JSON5 } from "bun";
     *
     * const result = JSON5.parse(`{
     *   // This is a comment
     *   name: 'my-app',
     *   version: '1.0.0', // trailing comma is allowed
     *   hex: 0xDEADbeef,
     *   half: .5,
     *   infinity: Infinity,
     * }`);
     * ```
     */
    export function parse(input: string): unknown;

    /**
     * Convert a JavaScript value into a JSON5 string. Object keys that are
     * valid identifiers are unquoted, strings use double quotes, `Infinity`
     * and `NaN` are represented as literals, and indented output includes
     * trailing commas.
     *
     * @category Utilities
     *
     * @param input The JavaScript value to stringify.
     * @param replacer Currently not supported.
     * @param space A number for how many spaces each level of indentation gets, or a string used as indentation.
     *              The number is clamped between 0 and 10, and the first 10 characters of the string are used.
     * @returns A JSON5 string, or `undefined` if the input is `undefined`, a function, or a symbol.
     *
     * @example
     * ```ts
     * import { JSON5 } from "bun";
     *
     * console.log(JSON5.stringify({ a: 1, b: "two" }));
     * // {a:1,b:"two"}
     *
     * console.log(JSON5.stringify({ a: 1, b: 2 }, null, 2));
     * // {
     * //   a: 1,
     * //   b: 2,
     * // }
     * ```
     */
    export function stringify(input: unknown, replacer?: undefined | null, space?: string | number): string | undefined;
  }

content.content:
922	  namespace YAML {
923	    /**
924	     * Parse a YAML string into a JavaScript value
925	     *
926	     * @category Utilities
927	     *
928	     * @param input The YAML string to parse
929	     * @returns A JavaScript value
930	     *
931	     * @example
932	     * ```ts
933	     * import { YAML } from "bun";
934	     *
935	     * console.log(YAML.parse("123")) // 123
936	     * console.log(YAML.parse("null")) // null
937	     * console.log(YAML.parse("false")) // false
938	     * console.log(YAML.parse("abc")) // "abc"
939	     * console.log(YAML.parse("- abc")) // [ "abc" ]
940	     * console.log(YAML.parse("abc: def")) // { "abc": "def" }
941	     * ```
942	     */
943	    export function parse(input: string): unknown;
944	
945	    /**
946	     * Convert a JavaScript value into a YAML string. Strings are double quoted if they contain keywords, non-printable or
947	     * escaped characters, or if a YAML parser would parse them as numbers. Anchors and aliases are inferred from objects, allowing cycles.
948	     *
949	     * @category Utilities
950	     *
951	     * @param input The JavaScript value to stringify.
952	     * @param replacer Currently not supported.
953	     * @param space A number for how many spaces each level of indentation gets, or a string used as indentation.
954	     *              Without this parameter, outputs flow-style (single-line) YAML.
955	     *              With this parameter, outputs block-style (multi-line) YAML.
956	     *              The number is clamped between 0 and 10, and the first 10 characters of the string are used.
957	     * @returns A string containing the YAML document.
958	     *
959	     * @example
960	     * ```ts
961	     * import { YAML } from "bun";
962	     *
963	     * const input = {
964	     *   abc: "def",
965	     *   num: 123
966	     * };
967	     *
968	     * // Without space - flow style (single-line)
969	     * console.log(YAML.stringify(input));
970	     * // {abc: def,num: 123}
971	     *
972	     * // With space - block style (multi-line)
973	     * console.log(YAML.stringify(input, null, 2));
974	     * // abc: def
975	     * // num: 123
976	     *
977	     * const cycle = {};
978	     * cycle.obj = cycle;
979	     * console.log(YAML.stringify(cycle, null, 2));
980	     * // &1
981	     * // obj: *1

file.content:
namespace YAML {
    /**
     * Parse a YAML string into a JavaScript value
     *
     * @category Utilities
     *
     * @param input The YAML string to parse
     * @returns A JavaScript value
     *
     * @example
     * ```ts
     * import { YAML } from "bun";
     *
     * console.log(YAML.parse("123")) // 123
     * console.log(YAML.parse("null")) // null
     * console.log(YAML.parse("false")) // false
     * console.log(YAML.parse("abc")) // "abc"
     * console.log(YAML.parse("- abc")) // [ "abc" ]
     * console.log(YAML.parse("abc: def")) // { "abc": "def" }
     * ```
     */
    export function parse(input: string): unknown;

    /**
     * Convert a JavaScript value into a YAML string. Strings are double quoted if they contain keywords, non-printable or
     * escaped characters, or if a YAML parser would parse them as numbers. Anchors and aliases are inferred from objects, allowing cycles.
     *
     * @category Utilities
     *
     * @param input The JavaScript value to stringify.
     * @param replacer Currently not supported.
     * @param space A number for how many spaces each level of indentation gets, or a string used as indentation.
     *              Without this parameter, outputs flow-style (single-line) YAML.
     *              With this parameter, outputs block-style (multi-line) YAML.
     *              The number is clamped between 0 and 10, and the first 10 characters of the string are used.
     * @returns A string containing the YAML document.
     *
     * @example
     * ```ts
     * import { YAML } from "bun";
     *
     * const input = {
     *   abc: "def",
     *   num: 123
     * };
     *
     * // Without space - flow style (single-line)
     * console.log(YAML.stringify(input));
     * // {abc: def,num: 123}
     *
     * // With space - block style (multi-line)
     * console.log(YAML.stringify(input, null, 2));
     * // abc: def
     * // num: 123
     *
     * const cycle = {};
     * cycle.obj = cycle;
     * console.log(YAML.stringify(cycle, null, 2));
     * // &1
     * // obj: *1

/**
   * INI related APIs
   */
  namespace INI {
    /**
     * Parse an INI string into a JavaScript object.
     *
     * Supports sections as nested objects, `key = value` pairs,
     * and comment lines starting with `;` or `#`. Values are
     * automatically converted to booleans, numbers, or null when
     * appropriate.
     *
     * @category Utilities
     *
     * @param input The INI string to parse
     * @returns A JavaScript object representing the parsed INI data
     *
     * @example
     * ```ts
     * import { INI } from "bun";
     *
     * const result = INI.parse(`
     * ; global settings
     * name = my-app
     * version = 1.0.0
     *
     * [database]
     * host = localhost
     * port = 5432
     * enabled = true
     * `);
     * // { name: "my-app", version: "1.0.0", database: { host: "localhost", port: 5432, enabled: true } }
     * ```
     */
    export function parse(input: string): Record<string, any>;

    /**
     * Convert a JavaScript object into an INI-formatted string.
     *
     * Top-level scalar properties are emitted as `key = value` pairs.
     * Nested objects become `[section]` headers with their own
     * key-value pairs underneath.
     *
     * @category Utilities
     *
     * @param input The JavaScript object to stringify
     * @returns An INI-formatted string
     *
     * @example
     * ```ts
     * import { INI } from "bun";
     *
     * const str = INI.stringify({
     *   name: "my-app",
     *   database: {
     *     host: "localhost",
     *     port: 5432,
     *   },
     * });
     * // name = my-app
     * //
     * // [database]
     * // host = localhost
     * // port = 5432
     * ```
     */
    export function stringify(input: Record<string, any>): string;
  }

  /**
   * TOML related APIs
   */
  namespace TOML {

content.text:
All changes are complete. Here's a summary of everything that was done:

## Changes Made

### 1. Created `src/bun.js/api/INIObject.zig` — the core implementation
- **`create`** — builds a JS object with `parse` and `stringify` methods (following the exact pattern of JSON5Object/YAMLObject with 2 properties)
- **`parse`** — takes a string, line-by-line parses INI format:
  - Handles `[section]` headers as nested objects
  - Handles `key = value` and `key=value` (whitespace around `=`)
  - Skips comment lines starting with `;` or `#`
  - Strips inline comments preceded by whitespace
  - Strips surrounding quotes (`"` or `'`)
  - Auto-converts values to booleans (`true`/`false`), `null`, integers, floats, or strings
  - Throws descriptive `SyntaxError` on unterminated sections, empty keys, missing `=`
  - Handles CRLF line endings
- **`stringify`** — takes a JS object, emits INI format:
  - Scalar properties as `key = value\n`
  - Nested objects as `[section]\nkey = value\n`
  - Two-pass approach: scalars first, then sections (with blank line separator)
  - Handles booleans, null, numbers, strings, and arrays (comma-separated)

### 2. Wired into `src/bun.js/api/BunObject.zig`
- Added `pub const INI = toJSLazyPropertyCallback(Bun.getINIObject);` in the BunObject struct
- Added `@export(&BunObject.INI, ...)` in `exportAll()`
- Added `getINIObject` function following the `getTOMLObject` pattern
- Added `const INIObject = bun.api.INIObject;` import

### 3. Updated `src/bun.js/api.zig`
- Added `pub const INIObject = @import("./api/INIObject.zig");`

### 4. Updated `src/bun.js/bindings/BunObject+exports.h`
- Added `macro(INI)` to `FOR_EACH_GETTER` in alphabetical order (between `Glob` and `JSON5`)

### 5. Updated `src/bun.js/bindings/BunObject.cpp`
- Added `INI BunObject_lazyPropCb_wrap_INI DontDelete|PropertyCallback` to the `bunObjectTable` hash table

### 6. Updated `packages/bun-types/bun.d.ts`
- Added `namespace INI` with JSDoc-documented `parse` and `stringify` functions, including usage examples

result:

## Changes Made

### 1. Created `src/bun.js/api/INIObject.zig` — the core implementation
- **`create`** — builds a JS object with `parse` and `stringify` methods (following the exact pattern of JSON5Object/YAMLObject with 2 properties)
- **`parse`** — takes a string, line-by-line parses INI format:
  - Handles `[section]` headers as nested objects
  - Handles `key = value` and `key=value` (whitespace around `=`)
  - Skips comment lines starting with `;` or `#`
  - Strips inline comments preceded by whitespace
  - Strips surrounding quotes (`"` or `'`)
  - Auto-converts values to booleans (`true`/`false`), `null`, integers, floats, or strings
  - Throws descriptive `SyntaxError` on unterminated sections, empty keys, missing `=`
  - Handles CRLF line endings
- **`stringify`** — takes a JS object, emits INI format:
  - Scalar properties as `key = value\n`
  - Nested objects as `[section]\nkey = value\n`
  - Two-pass approach: scalars first, then sections (with blank line separator)
  - Handles booleans, null, numbers, strings, and arrays (comma-separated)

### 2. Wired into `src/bun.js/api/BunObject.zig`
- Added `pub const INI = toJSLazyPropertyCallback(Bun.getINIObject);` in the BunObject struct
- Added `@export(&BunObject.INI, ...)` in `exportAll()`
- Added `getINIObject` function following the `getTOMLObject` pattern
- Added `const INIObject = bun.api.INIObject;` import

### 3. Updated `src/bun.js/api.zig`
- Added `pub const INIObject = @import("./api/INIObject.zig");`

### 4. Updated `src/bun.js/bindings/BunObject+exports.h`
- Added `macro(INI)` to `FOR_EACH_GETTER` in alphabetical order (between `Glob` and `JSON5`)

### 5. Updated `src/bun.js/bindings/BunObject.cpp`
- Added `INI BunObject_lazyPropCb_wrap_INI DontDelete|PropertyCallback` to the `bunObjectTable` hash table

### 6. Updated `packages/bun-types/bun.d.ts`
